skill.name: data-validator
skill.description: Validate datasets against schemas, business rules, format patterns, and data quality checks.
skill.summary: Build validation checks and reports for structured data quality workflows.
skill.author: CuriousLearner

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, sensitive-file, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found these are false positives from Markdown code fences and benign validation examples in SKILL.md, with no prompt injection or malicious behavior found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to Markdown fenced examples and language markers, not executable Ruby or shell backticks. The skill contains instructional snippets only and does not define an execution hook.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations align with Markdown code fences and example sections. No command runner, subprocess call, or shell invocation is present in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Validation Examples Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings refer to schema, dataframe, regex, and reporting operations used for data validation. They inspect user-provided data structures, not the host system or network.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code context shows local validation logic over provided records and columns. I did not find evidence of host discovery, port scanning, network probing, or environment enumeration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network and Sensitive Data Terms in Benign Validators
low_findings.description: The network finding is an API request validation example without an HTTP client call. Password and URL terms appear in format validators and sample business rules, not credential access or exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines validate password strength, URL strings, and local endpoint schema selection. No credential file read, secret harvesting, HTTP client, or outbound request was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Heuristic
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding points to the YAML description line. That line describes data quality standards and contains no cryptographic algorithm or implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is metadata text only. I did not find evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic code in the reviewed context.

content.value_statement: Bad data breaks products, analytics, and compliance workflows. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design validation checks, explain failures, and draft quality reports.
actual_capabilities: Draft JSON Schema validation logic with detailed error reporting.
Design dataframe schema checks for columns, types, nulls, uniqueness, ranges, and patterns.
Create business rule validators for records, orders, registrations, and domain constraints.
Suggest data quality checks for completeness, duplicates, outliers, and format patterns.
Generate validation report structures with quality scores and fix guidance.
limitations: It provides guidance and example code, not a packaged validator library.
It does not execute validation unless the host tool runs generated code separately.
Generated rules still need review against real business requirements.
Large datasets may require streaming or distributed validation outside the examples.

use_cases.title: Check API Payloads
use_cases.description: Create schema rules and clear error messages for request and response validation.

use_cases.title: Audit Dataset Quality
use_cases.description: Identify missing values, duplicates, outliers, and invalid formats before analysis.

use_cases.title: Document Business Rules
use_cases.description: Turn policy constraints into testable validation logic and readable reports.

prompt_templates.title: Validate a Simple File
prompt_templates.prompt: Use data-validator to review this dataset against my schema. List each validation failure with the field, rule, and suggested fix.

prompt_templates.title: Create Quality Checks
prompt_templates.prompt: Use data-validator to design checks for completeness, duplicate rows, format validity, and numeric outliers in this table.

prompt_templates.title: Map Business Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Use data-validator to convert these business requirements into validation rules, edge cases, and failure messages.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Validation Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use data-validator to design a validation report for this pipeline, including quality dimensions, scoring, and remediation priorities.

output_examples.input: A customer file has missing emails, duplicate customer IDs, and ages outside the accepted range.
output_examples.output: The report identifies required-field failures, duplicate key conflicts, and range violations. It groups errors by severity and recommends correcting IDs first.

output_examples.input: An order API payload must match totals, require shipping addresses for physical goods, and require emails for digital goods.
output_examples.output: The skill produces business rules for totals, shipping requirements, and digital delivery contacts. Each rule includes a readable failure message.

output_examples.input: A product team needs validation coverage before importing a vendor catalog.
output_examples.output: The skill proposes checks for required columns, data types, price ranges, URL formats, duplicates, and a summary quality score.

best_practices: Define schemas and business rules before writing validation logic.
Return specific errors that name the field, rule, and failed value.
Test validators with valid records, invalid records, empty data, and boundary values.
anti_patterns: Do not treat format validation as proof that data is correct.
Do not hide validation failures behind a single generic error message.
Do not run generated validation code on sensitive data without review.

faq.question: Can this skill validate JSON data?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides JSON Schema validation patterns and detailed error reporting examples.

faq.question: Can it validate CSV or dataframe data?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes patterns for column checks, data types, nulls, duplicates, ranges, and formats.

faq.question: Does it run validation automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides guidance and example code. The host tool must run any generated code separately.

faq.question: Can it handle custom business rules?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows how to express domain rules as explicit validators with clear error messages.

faq.question: Is it suitable for compliance workflows?
faq.answer: It can help draft validation reports, but compliance requirements need expert review.

faq.question: Does it send data to external services?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed skill file contains local validation examples only.
